trade.summary: Growing bilateral trade. Switzerland exports pharmaceuticals, watches, and precision instruments. South Korea exports electronics, vehicles, and ships. An EFTA-Korea FTA has been in place since 2006.

diplomatic.summary: Friendly and stable relations. Both countries are advanced democracies with strong commitment to multilateralism. Switzerland has historically played a role representing interests in North-South Korean diplomacy through its neutrality. Cooperation in WTO, WHO, and other Geneva-based organizations.

military.summary: No formal military alliance. Switzerland's neutrality is a limiting factor. Switzerland has historically served as a neutral member in the Neutral Nations Supervisory Commission (NNSC) on the Korean Peninsula, a unique security role.

financial.summary: Moderate bilateral investment. Korean conglomerates (chaebols) have Swiss operations. Swiss pharmaceutical and financial firms operate in Korea. Growing cross-border investment in technology sectors.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Some cooperation on nuclear energy technology and clean energy transition.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation in semiconductors, biotech, and materials science. Swiss and Korean universities maintain exchange programs. Joint publications increasing in electronics and pharmaceutical research.
